title: Aletheia run evidence meta file
description: Schema for <evidence_dir>/<name>_<YYYY-MM-DD>/meta.json — the mandatory record every kept campaign writes (see the evidence-convention skill). Generalized from a working research repository's campaign meta files. Extra fields are allowed and encouraged.

task.description: What this campaign is, with a pointer to the plan item / task contract it executes.

status.description: Campaign lifecycle state, e.g. RUNNING, COMPLETE, ABORTED, EVIDENCE_PROMOTED.

git_tree_clean_at_launch.description: Whether the working tree was clean at launch; if false, record per-script blob hashes below.

config_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the file defining the canonical defaults the run assumed (see canonical-params / run-provenance).

config_path.description: Path of the hashed defaults file.

inputs.description: Fingerprints of every dataset/file the run read (see run-provenance).

path.description: Path actually opened by the code.

scripts.description: Identity of executed entry points (git blob hashes recover exact versions from a dirty tree).

arch_native_verified.description: Interpreter architecture matches the machine (no emulation).

engines.description: Domain-engine versions (solver/simulator/training framework) keyed by name.

hardware.description: Hardware context (see numerical-determinism). Required for any timing claim.

derivation.description: Rule mapping realization index to seed.

rng.description: Library + algorithm, when the library offers several.

parameters.description: Parameters/grid actually run (free-form; axes as arrays).

expectation.description: Pre-registered prediction written BEFORE launch — direction, rough magnitude, and any pre-declared branches (see research-methodology).

outputs.description: Output files this campaign produced (relative to the run directory).

not_done.description: Explicitly skipped/truncated portions — silent truncation is forbidden (evidence-convention).

provenance_note.description: Free-text honesty note: what was waived, promoted, or unusual about this run.
